artifact: IEC 62304 Traceability Matrix (variant A: evidence array per requirement)

rows.requirement_text: The checker returns an InteractionResult(outcome, direction) pair, never a bare boolean. outcome is one of NoInteractionExpected, Caution, CautionLowRelevance, Avoid, Contraindicated, DoseReductionAdvised, NotCovered; direction is BleedingRisk, ThrombosisRisk, NoRisk, or UnknownRisk - every one of the source's 17 sections is headed by exactly this distinction.

evidence.caveat: Formally proven against the stated specification.
evidence.proof_content_caveat: Definitional proof: the postcondition restates the implementation (ensures is equivalent to the body), so the proof obligation is a tautology discharged by reflexivity. Certified: totality, type-safety, match-exhaustiveness, and the literal boundary structure of the definition. NOT certified: an independent property beyond the definition, or fidelity to the source requirement.

rows.requirement_text: CheckInteraction(doac, agent, hasOtherBleedingRiskFactors, treatmentIndication) is a total, hardcoded pairwise lookup covering 17 of the source's 17 named sections. No precondition at all as of REQ-DDI-5 (2026-07-12) - the four apixaban+{Rifampicin, Carbamazepine, Phenytoin, Phenobarbital} cells this function used to exclude are now provable for every constructible TreatmentIndication value, so the exclusion was removed rather than kept as dead code.

rows.requirement_text: SSRIs/SNRIs: dabigatran's dose-reduction advice is conditional on a caller-supplied hasOtherBleedingRiskFactors flag - both branches proven, not just the one exercised first.

rows.requirement_text: Fail-safe: any (doac, agent) pair not present in the source's named set - including within-source gaps like apixaban+dronedarone - returns NotCovered, never silently NoInteractionExpected.

rows.requirement_text: Rifampicin and carbamazepine/phenytoin/phenobarbital both make apixaban's outcome depend on a third axis, clinical indication - TreatmentIndication (AFStrokePrevention | RecurrentVTEPrevention | OrthopaedicVTEProphylaxis, the third constructor added 2026-07-13 for REQ-DDI-6's own indication scoping, not apixaban's - see REQ-DDI-6 below). Both named indications give apixaban the identical "use with caution" outcome, so every constructible input is provable - CheckInteraction's precondition (REQ-DDI-2) is removed entirely as a result. For the orthopaedic-VTE-prophylaxis indication specifically (added for REQ-DDI-6, not sourced for apixaban here), all four apixaban+inducer cells return NotCovered rather than a fabricated Caution - fixed 2026-07-13 after a code review found the original implementation's match arms computed Caution unconditionally, without inspecting treatmentIndication at all, silently returning an unproven outcome once the third constructor made that indication constructible. Matches (Apixaban, Dronedarone)'s established silent-cell convention: the interaction source never states an apixaban outcome for the hip/knee-replacement indication, so NotCovered is the honest answer, not a value this function should fabricate.

rows.requirement_text: The DoseReductionAdvised outcome kind is proven by CheckInteraction itself; DoseReductionTargetMg proves the specific numeric mg figure for the five real cells the source states one for: Dabigatran+Verapamil (110mg twice daily), and Edoxaban+{Dronedarone, ErythromycinSystemic, Ketoconazole, Ciclosporin} (30mg each, sourced individually per row). Dabigatran+SSRIOrSNRI is deliberately excluded, permanently, not staged for a future v3 - the source gives no mg figure for that cell ("as per the Summary of Product Characteristics," no number stated on this page). Apixaban never appears in this function's precondition at all - a direct, confirmed consequence of CheckInteraction never producing DoseReductionAdvised for apixaban anywhere in its match arms, not a hand-written exclusion. The Dabigatran+Verapamil cell is indication-scoped (added 2026-07-13): the source states the 110mg figure applies only for AF-stroke-prevention and DVT/PE-prevention-and-treatment, not dabigatran's third, real, UK-licensed indication (primary VTE prevention after elective hip/knee replacement surgery, confirmed via sources/emc-smpc-dabigatran-indications-2025.md) - a genuine source-silent cell for that third indication, now provably excluded rather than silently unaddressed. The four Edoxaban cells stay deliberately indication-free - the source states no indication scope for those rows.
